tags.name: Pantai Merah
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Shore entry reef on eastern Komodo Island. Depth corrected 30m→25m (sources confirm sandy base at 25m, most life at 5-15m). Site type reef confirmed. Difficulty Beginner confirmed.

tags.name: Manta Point dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Central Komodo manta cleaning/feeding station. Shallow sandy bottom 3-15m. Beginner accessible when currents mild. Depth 15m confirmed. Site type drift confirmed (tidal flow drives manta activity).

tags.name: Mawan
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type changed reef→drift. Mawan is primarily a manta cleaning station drift dive west of Siaba Besar. Depth corrected 24m→25m (sources: 5-25m range). Mandarin fish description in previous markdown was unsupported — Wainilu is the mandarin fish site. Mawan confirmed for manta rays, eagle rays, bamboo sharks, whitetip sharks, turtles.

tags.name: Crystal Bay Dive Site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: CRITICAL CORRECTION. Previous markdown described Nusa Penida's Crystal Bay (mola mola site, coordinates ~-8.71, 115.45) — this is a different location 400+km away. This site (coordinates -8.447, 119.568) is a bay near Gili Lawa Darat in NORTHERN KOMODO. Confirmed as a beginner-friendly site with sandy bottom, no current, used for checkout dives and courses. Difficulty corrected Intermediate→Beginner. Depth corrected 30m→15m. All mola mola content removed as hallucination.

tags.name: Pengah Kecil
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type pinnacle confirmed. Depth 27m confirmed. Difficulty Intermediate confirmed. Confirmed marine life: white-tip reef sharks, hawksbill turtles, giant moray eels, pygmy seahorses, whip coral shrimp, nudibranchs, frogfish, giant trevally, bumphead parrotfish, eagle rays.

tags.name: Siaba Besar dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed. Depth 20m confirmed (sources: 12-22m). Difficulty Beginner confirmed. Known as 'Turtle City'. Confirmed species: green turtles, cuttlefish, white-tip sharks, blue-spotted stingrays, frogfish, ghost pipefish. Rare sightings of dugongs and silver-tip sharks noted by Blue Marlin Dive.

tags.name: Crystal Rock Dive Site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type pinnacle confirmed. Depth corrected 30m→35m (sources confirm 15-35m, some say tops out 3-5m below surface). Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Location corrected in markdown from 'Gili Motang area' to 'Gili Lawa Laut area'. Confirmed marine life: grey/white/black-tip sharks, giant trevally, tuna, barracuda, napoleon wrasse, eagle rays, pygmy seahorses, nudibranchs.

tags.name: The Cauldron Dive Site
tags.scuba_diving:name: The Cauldron
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type drift confirmed. Depth 22-25m confirmed. Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Blue Marlin confirms two distinct halves: bowl-shaped side with gorgonian fans and canyon system. Manta rays confirmed feeding in pass during current flow.

tags.name: Batu Tiga Dive Site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type pinnacle confirmed (three rock pinnacles). Depth corrected 30m→35m (Blue Marlin: 15-35m). Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Confirmed species: sharks, barracuda schools, snapper, batfish, trevally, bumpheads, fusiliers, nudibranchs. Blue Marlin notes this is the least frequently dived Komodo site with pristine corals.

tags.name: Bonsai dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type corrected reef→wall (overhangs, caves, valleys, swim-throughs to 40m confirmed by multiple sources; also known as 'One Tree' or 'Lonely Tree'). Difficulty corrected Intermediate→Advanced (sources: 'mostly for advanced divers', 'extremely dangerous currents'). Depth corrected 20m→40m. Confirmed marine life: emperor sweetlips, napoleon wrasse, hawksbill turtles, grouper in overhangs; giant red and white fan corals.

tags.name: 3 Sisters
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type pinnacle confirmed (three submerged pinnacles in southern Komodo). Depth corrected 30m→40m (Blue Marlin: 15-40m). Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Blue Marlin confirms pinnacles 10m apart with 20m sand base rising 3-5m; strong currents. ScubaBoard confirms as 'stunning and diverse' with abundant fish life and sharks.

tags.name: Sabayor Kecil
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type corrected reef→wall. Sea-Undersea confirms 'steep rock bank' dropping to 30-40m with strong tidal currents. Confirmed species: stingrays, Napoleon wrasse, groupers. Difficulty Intermediate confirmed. Depth 30m confirmed (wall drops to 30-40m, max recreational depth 30m).

tags.name: Mini Wall
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type wall confirmed. Depth 30m confirmed. Difficulty Intermediate confirmed. Blue Marlin confirms dramatic 30m+ wall drop with spotted eagle rays and crocodile flathead in sandy channels.

tags.name: Pillarsteen
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type corrected wall→pinnacle (Blue Marlin confirms as a pinnacle with caves and swim-throughs, slopes with fusiliers and soft corals, 18-40m). Difficulty corrected Intermediate→Advanced (Blue Marlin: Advanced only; ScubaBoard confirms night dive depth 20-24m). Depth 40m confirmed.

tags.name: Tatawa Besar dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed. Depth 25m confirmed (sources: 15-25m). Difficulty Beginner confirmed (all sources agree: Open Water friendly, suitable for all levels). Confirmed marine life: green turtles, blacktip sharks, nudibranchs, shrimp, seahorses, staghorn coral fields.

tags.name: Tatawa Kecil dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type drift confirmed. Depth corrected 30m→35m (Blue Marlin: 15-35m). Difficulty Advanced confirmed (all sources: Advanced/very experienced only). Confirmed: swim-throughs, valleys, giant boulders, best at slack/rising tide. Confirmed species: groupers, snappers, sweetlips, jacks, sharks, eagle rays (Dragon Dive), giant trevallies, mobulas (Dragon Dive).

tags.name: Indihiang
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type wall confirmed (steeply dropping walls, drift-diving). Depth 25m confirmed. Difficulty Advanced confirmed (strong currents, plan around slack tide). Confirmed marine life: potato cod, large napoleon wrasse, schools of giant trevally and snapper.

tags.name: Gaja Rock dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Could not find 3 independent sources with site-specific details. Site type pinnacle and depth 20m plausible based on name and location in southern Komodo, but not independently confirmed. NOT marked validated. Search results only returned generic Komodo dive site pages without Gaja Rock specifics.

tags.name: Loh Namu
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed (sloping reef/wall, dive around the point south to north). Depth corrected 20m→28m (source: 'depth of about 28m'). Difficulty Intermediate confirmed. Confirmed marine life: leaf scorpionfish, blue ribbon eels, crocodile fish, nudibranch, giant clam at 8m.

tags.name: Pink Beach
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed. Depth corrected 30m→25m (sandy base at 25-30m, most life at 5-15m). Difficulty Beginner confirmed. Entry corrected boat→both (shore entry confirmed by multiple sources). Pink sand from red coral fragments confirmed. Noted as separate from Pantai Merah.

tags.name: Wainilu
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type muck confirmed. Depth corrected 12m→25m (Dragon Dive confirms max 30m; Blue Marlin: 15-25m avg; Scuba Junkie: plateaus at 25m). Difficulty corrected Beginner→Intermediate (requires excellent buoyancy control per Blue Marlin; Dragon Dive says 'all levels' but notes buoyancy requirement). Confirmed species: flamboyant cuttlefish, mandarinfish, mimic octopus, frogfish, ornate ghost pipefish, wunderpus, blue-ring octopus, bobtail squid, harlequin shrimp, ribbon eels. Best at dusk for mandarinfish mating.

tags.name: Loh Sera dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Could not find 3 independent sources with site-specific details. Wall type and Advanced difficulty are consistent with site's position on exposed SW Komodo coast. Site appears primarily on extended liveaboard itineraries with very limited online coverage. NOT marked validated.

tags.name: Tanjung Loh Sera dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type confirmed as pinnacle/wall combo (submerged rocks and pinnacles forming valleys, wall closer to shore). Depth corrected 20m→30m (sources: 5-30m range). Difficulty Advanced confirmed (strong currents, experienced divers only for pinnacles). Confirmed species: dogtooth tuna, turtles, giant trevally, potato cod, bumphead parrotfish, malabar grouper, napoleon wrasse, manta rays.

tags.name: Gindang
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed (sloping soft coral garden). Depth 20m confirmed. Difficulty Intermediate confirmed. Confirmed species per Scuba Junkie: blue-ringed octopus, flamboyant cuttlefish, ribbon eels, seahorses. Note: marine life claims in existing markdown (napoleon wrasse, giant trevally, reef sharks) are generic Komodo regional attribution — Scuba Junkie confirms this as a soft coral garden macro site.

tags.name: Payong
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Could not find 3 independent sources with site-specific details for Payong. Site name found in Komodo dive site lists but no operator pages with specific descriptions located. Site type reef and Intermediate difficulty are plausible but unconfirmed. NOT marked validated.

tags.name: Sabolon besar dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed. Depth corrected 20m→22m (Blue Marlin: 12-22m). Difficulty corrected Intermediate→Beginner (multiple sources: Beginners, suitable for check-out dives, all levels, snorkellers). Confirmed species: large schools of reef fish, turtles, cuttlefish, giant sea fans, macro subjects including mantis shrimp and pygmy seahorses.

tags.name: Sabolon kecil dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type corrected reef→pinnacle (seamount topping at ~22m confirmed by SSI MyDiveGuide and Wet Frog Divers). Depth confirmed 22m (seamount top). Difficulty Intermediate confirmed (nice drift dives around the seamount). Confirmed species: sea fans, hard corals, schools of barracudas circling the pinnacle, turtles, white-tip reef sharks.

tags.name: Lighthouse Reef Dive Site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed (two pinnacles off sloping reef). Depth corrected 20m→35m (Blue Marlin: 15-35m). Difficulty corrected Intermediate→Advanced (Blue Marlin: Advanced only). Confirmed species: red tooth triggerfish, white-tip reef sharks, bumphead parrotfish, sweetlips, frogfish, spawning Indonesian groupers, occasional dolphins (Blue Marlin). Pygmy seahorses on sea fans confirmed (multiple sources).

tags.name: Sebayur dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type reef confirmed. Depth 20m confirmed. Difficulty Beginner confirmed. DiveHappy confirms steep white sand slope, protected bay, leaf fish, barrel sponges, reef fish. PADI confirms beginner-friendly. SSI confirms multiple reef species. Site has a seamount at ~25m (noted by SSI) but main reef is 20m.

tags.name: Seraya kecil dive site
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type corrected reef→muck (black-sand and rubble slope muck site close to Labuan Bajo, well documented as macro/muck diving destination). However, the specific dive site at these coordinates (-8.418, 119.862) is near Seraya village on Flores, just outside park boundary. Muck/macro character is consistent. Could not find 3 independent sources with site-specific details confirming this exact location. NOT marked validated. Depth 20m plausible for black sand slope.

tags.name: Makassar Reef (Manta Point)
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Site type drift confirmed. Depth corrected 15m→18m (Blue Marlin: 12-18m; Dragon Dive: main action 6-18m). Difficulty Beginner confirmed (Open Water accessible per Dragon Dive and Blue Marlin). Also known as Karang Makassar / Takat Makassar. Dragon Dive confirms up to 40-50 mantas, 2.5km long reef plateau, multiple cleaning stations. Confirmed species: reef mantas, bamboo sharks, eagle rays, giant clams, bumphead parrotfish, turtles.

tags.name: Big Nose
tags.description: Dive site in the north Komodo area. Site details unverified — contributed from a diver's personal logbook. Located in open water northwest of Komodo Island. May be known by a different name among local operators.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: No site-specific sources found. Could not find 3 independent sources. NOT marked validated. Name not found in any operator's site listing.

tags.name: Golden Passage
tags.description: A high-energy drift dive through the narrow channel between Gili Lawa Darat and Komodo Island. Tidal currents reaching several knots sweep divers past volcanic rock walls covered in hard and soft corals. Schools of trevally, whitetip and blacktip reef sharks, hawksbill turtles, and occasional manta rays gather in the nutrient-rich flow.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Site type drift confirmed. Depth 25m confirmed. Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Description reviewed — marine life claims are consistent with current-swept northern Komodo channel diving. No hallucinations detected.

tags.name: Castle Rock
tags.description: A large underwater seamount north of Gili Lawa Laut with its peak just meters below the surface. Blanketed in soft corals and sea fans hosting pygmy seahorses. Medium to very strong currents draw huge schools of fusiliers, whitetip and grey reef sharks, giant trevally, napoleon wrasse, and occasional eagle rays. Widely considered one of Komodo's best dive sites.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Site type pinnacle confirmed (seamount from 75m to 3-4m surface). Depth 35m confirmed. Difficulty Advanced confirmed. Marine life cross-checked: grey/white/black-tip sharks, tuna, barracuda, giant trevallies confirmed by Blue Marlin and Dragon Dive. Pygmy seahorses on soft corals confirmed by Blue Marlin. No conflicts found.

tags.name: Batu Bolong
tags.description: An exposed rock pinnacle rising from over 70m in the center of Lintah Strait. Strong currents hit alternating sides depending on tide. Every inch of the pinnacle is covered in healthy coral, hosting enormous densities of anthias, reef sharks, giant trevally, dogtooth tuna, and napoleon wrasse. Regularly ranked among the world's top dive sites.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Depth corrected 30m→40m (Blue Marlin: 15-40m; Dragon Dive: descends to 85m, recreational max 40m). Pinnacle confirmed in 75m water. CNN #27 ranking confirmed by Blue Marlin. Marine life cross-checked: reef sharks, humphead wrasse, dogtooth tuna, turtles all confirmed. No conflicts found.

tags.name: Secret Garden
tags.description: A sheltered bay reef off northeast Padar Island with mild currents. Features large green and orange black coral bushes, vibrant soft corals, and rich macro life including mantis shrimp, ribbon eels, blue-ringed octopus, ghost pipefish, and nudibranchs. A calm alternative to Komodo's current-swept northern sites.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Dragon Dive confirms as macro paradise and mobula/ray migration corridor. Marine life: black coral bushes, soft corals, mantis shrimp, ribbon eels, blue-ringed octopus, ghost pipefish, nudibranchs are consistent with protected bay macro sites in Komodo. No specific independent confirmation of blue-ringed octopus at this exact site — flagged as possible generic regional attribution but not removed (consistent with similar Komodo macro sites).

tags.name: Padar Bay
tags.description: A protected bay on the south side of Padar offering sheltered reef diving with gentle slopes and coral gardens. Calm conditions make it ideal for less experienced divers and a relaxing complement to the more demanding sites in central and northern Komodo.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Previous validation_source was only 'divekomodo.com + ScubaBoard user Dan GPS' — insufficient (2 sources, one GPS only). Could not find 3 independent sources with site-specific Padar Bay details. NOT marked validated.

tags.name: Crinoid Wall
tags.description: A colorful wall in a small cove near the southern entrance to Loh Dasami at Nusa Kode, almost directly opposite Yellow Wall. Crevices hold sleeping slipper lobsters, cleaner shrimp, decorator crabs, red sea apples, and a great variety of tunicates and soft corals. An excellent slow-paced macro dive best done in the morning.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Also known as 'Crinoid Corner'. Wall type confirmed. Depth 25m confirmed (starts at 25m, ascends slowly). Multiple sources confirm: slipper lobsters, cleaner shrimp, decorator crabs, sea cucumbers, Spanish dancers, bamboo sharks resting in crevices. Morning dive timing confirmed. No conflicts.

tags.name: Yellow Wall
tags.description: A dramatic wall along the east coast of Nusa Kode named for the profusion of bright yellow soft corals (Tubastrea) and robust sea cucumbers. Crevices harbor nudibranchs and ladybug amphipods, with clown triggerfish, fairy basslets, and occasional sharks and mantas cruising past. Best dived in the afternoon for optimal light. Medium to strong currents.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Wall confirmed on east coast of Nusa Kode. Depth 40m confirmed. Difficulty Advanced confirmed (medium to strong currents per multiple sources). Confirmed species: yellow soft corals (Tubastrea), sea cucumbers, featherstars (Dive Happy), fork-tailed fairy basslets, clown triggerfish, butterflyfish, whitetip reef sharks, green turtles, red snappers. Afternoon best light confirmed. 'Ladybug amphipods' claim unverified across sources — removed from description as unconfirmed micro-specific claim.

tags.name: Cannibal Rock
tags.description: A small seamount in Loh Dasami Bay at Nusa Kode, carpeted in dense soft corals, crinoids, and sponges. Widely regarded as one of Indonesia's premier macro and critter diving sites. Home to an extraordinary density of invertebrate life including nudibranchs, sea apples, and crinoid shrimp, with occasional manta rays and reef sharks passing by.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Site type: Cannibal Rock is a pinnacle/seamount rather than classic muck — it is carpeted in soft corals and sponges (not sand/rubble). However 'muck' is used loosely in Komodo to mean critter/macro diving. Depth 5-35m confirmed. Confirmed species: nudibranchs, rhinopias, frogfish, pygmy seahorses in sponges and sea fans (divenet.com), magnificent anemones, sea apples (Pseudocolchirus violaceus). 'Occasional manta rays' claim is consistent with proximity to Nusa Kode area. No conflicts.

tags.name: Boulders
tags.description: Large coral-encrusted boulders on the southwestern tip of Nusa Kode creating habitat for giant potato cod, Malabar groupers, bamboo sharks, and schools of giant trevally and fusiliers. Coleman shrimp on fire urchins and rare paddleflap scorpionfish (Rhinopia) attract macro photographers. Also a popular night dive.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Lotus Liveaboards confirms location in Loh Dasami Bay, Nusa Kode. Large boulders from 30m to 8m. Giant groupers, potato cod, malabar groupers confirmed. 'Rhinopia (paddleflap scorpionfish)' confirmed by multiple Nusa Kode macro sources. Coleman shrimp on fire urchins consistent with site type. No conflicts.

tags.name: Torpedo Alley
tags.description: A black sand slope in Horseshoe Bay at Nusa Kode and Komodo's most celebrated night dive. Named for the torpedo rays (electric rays) that blend into the substrate. After dark the slope comes alive with ambon scorpionfish, frogfish, stargazers, snake eels, cuttlefish, and abundant nudibranchs. Equally rewarding during the day for muck diving enthusiasts.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. Lotus Liveaboards confirms location in Horseshoe Bay near southern Rinca Island. Black sand slope confirmed. Torpedo rays (electric rays) as namesake confirmed by Lotus. 'Ambon scorpionfish' is a specific species claim — consistent with Komodo muck sites but not individually cross-checked against this specific location; flagged for future verification. Frogfish, stargazers, snake eels, cuttlefish, nudibranchs consistent with muck slope habitat.

tags.name: Manta Alley
tags.description: A rocky channel at the far southern tip of Komodo Island where manta rays aggregate at cleaning stations. Peak season: sources conflict — divehappy.com reports mantas year-round with best visibility September-March; Dragon Dive Komodo reports peak November-June for southern Komodo. Sightings of multiple mantas on a single dive are common in peak season. Strong currents and cold thermoclines (22-23°C) make this a challenging dive. Shallow cleaning stations at 5-15m; deeper sandy channels to ~20-25m. Maximum depth 40m.
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-10: Re-audited. DiveHappy confirms rocky channel at southern Komodo. Channel structure confirmed (DiveHappy: 'an alley hewn through rock'). Cleaning stations confirmed. Cold thermoclines (22-23°C) confirmed by DiveHappy. Seasonal conflict updated: Dragon Dive Komodo now lists November-June as best for southern Komodo; DiveHappy does not give a single peak month. Description updated to reflect current source data. Previous 'mobula rays' claim removed — DiveHappy does not confirm mobulas specifically at this site. 'Sweetlips and giant trevallies' from previous version not confirmed by DiveHappy specifically — removed to avoid generic regional padding.
